summary: Frontend-only responsive regression run completed across mobile/tablet/desktop for login, dashboard, new session, and session detail. Found a blocking horizontal overflow on dashboard at small widths; dark mode toggles still function correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Horizontal overflow/cutoff on mobile (390px) and tablet (768px). Root document scroll width exceeds viewport; sidebar/cards/CTA render beyond right edge.

issues: Primary CTA (Create New Session) starts off-screen at small widths due to horizontal layout expansion.
Content appears cut off to the right until user scrolls horizontally.

action_items: Fix dashboard small-width overflow so documentElement.scrollWidth never exceeds clientWidth on 390px and 768px.
Re-check AppShell + Dashboard sizing constraints (likely grid item min-width/content min-width interaction) and keep CTA in viewport without horizontal scroll.
Run responsive retest across /login, /, /sessions/new, /sessions/:id after the fix.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard table uses min-w-[700px]; combined with current AppShell/grid sizing this can force page-level horizontal overflow on narrow breakpoints instead of local table scroll containment.
AppShell content region lacks defensive min-w-0 on grid children, which can allow intrinsic child width to push layout beyond viewport in responsive mode.
seed_data_creation: None in this run (reused existing test account and existing session).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Playwright responsive checks already ran across 390/768/1920. Login, new session, and session detail had no horizontal overflow. Dashboard overflow reproduced consistently on both 390 and 768 widths with off-screen CTA and widened shell content.
rca of the issue: Repro: login -> open / -> set viewport 390x844 or 768x1024. Observed documentElement.scrollWidth > clientWidth (390->768, 768->776). Offending nodes include sidebar/main/dashboard sections and CTA shifted right. Likely cause is intrinsic min-width pressure (Dashboard min-w-[700px] table and/or missing min-w-0 constraints on AppShell grid children), causing page-level expansion instead of contained horizontal scrolling.
